✓ Simple English mode is on
嫘祖學院

Leizu Academy

嫘祖 was the consort of the Yellow Emperor, credited with teaching the world to cultivate silk. The school takes its name from her, and its method from the same patience.

· · · · A practice within Seminar Schools · cloud-growth view →
i. The program

One arrow, two birds 一箭雙鵰

Leizu Academy teaches five subjects as one curriculum. 歷史 History. 英文 English. 哲學 Philosophy. 媒體 Media Literacy. 社會研究 Social Studies. They are taught together because they ask versions of the same question.

一箭雙鵰. One arrow takes two birds. Each session does double work. A History session reads a primary source and trains the student's writing on the same page. Whatever the student needs the work to do, the work does it.

Students arrive having read. The session moves through claims, counterclaims, and implications. The teacher hosts the inquiry. The teacher does not deliver its conclusion. The argument that emerges is one neither side brought into the room intact.

ii. The five elements

Five subjects 五科

Each subject inherits one of the five elements as its symbolic anchor. Wood feeds fire, fire makes earth, earth bears metal, metal carries water, water grows wood. The cycle is the curriculum.

iii. Subjects in detail

What is taught

Click any course to add it. The total updates as you go. The catalog is exhaustive. You decide what matters. Pick a route below to filter by goal: Forest year, IB Diploma, or OSSD prep.

Path:
IB Diploma capstone work

The IB awards the diploma on the basis of six subject scores plus three core requirements. The courses above prepare the subject scores. These four pieces of capstone work are where the diploma gets won or lost.

知識論
TOK Essay
A 1,600-word essay on one of six prescribed titles. Worth two-thirds of the TOK grade. Coached over five sessions: framing, claim-counterclaim architecture, drafting, revision, final polish.
擴展論文
Extended Essay
A 4,000-word independent research essay. Coached over eight sessions: topic, research question, methodology, sources, drafting, revision, supervisor reflection sessions, final submission.
內部評估
Internal Assessment support
Most IB subjects have an Internal Assessment worth 20 to 30% of the final grade. Targeted coaching for whichever IAs the student is writing. Methodology, structure, analysis, conclusion.
大學申請
University admissions writing
Personal statements, supplementary essays, scholarship applications. The IB diploma is your transcript; the application essays are how universities meet you. Coached on Common App, OUAC, UCAS, and direct US/UK applications.
Completing the IB route qualifies students for the Seminar year. That route is roughly 30 subject courses plus these four capstones.
OSSD curriculum coaching

The Ontario Secondary School Diploma awards credits across grades 9-12 plus the Ontario Secondary School Literacy Test plus 40 community involvement hours. Course coaching above aligns with the Ontario curriculum codes shown beside each course.

A student doing the OSSD curriculum at their home school typically books one to two sessions per week per subject during the school year, focusing on assessments and the culminating tasks. Completing 20 sessions of one-on-one work qualifies for the Seminar year.
The Forest year

A full year inside the practice. Three sessions a week across all five subjects. The courses highlighted above are the foundational set that constitutes a balanced Forest year. Pick at least one course from each pillar.

144 contact hours across the year. A written account of the student's development at year-end, suitable for sharing with universities. Replies on Forest year inquiries within twenty-four hours.
iv. Format

How sessions run

Sessions run online over Google Meet. Each session is two periods of focused work, 40 to 45 minutes each, separated by a 5 to 10 minute break. The total is around 90 minutes. The break consolidates what just happened before more goes in. Cadence is weekly during the school year, concentrated before exams or deadlines. Two formats below.

Private sessions
One student, one teacher. The full session is devoted to that student's work. New students start here.
Seminar sessions
A small group of students with one teacher, working through the same text together. Half the per-session rate. Available after 20 to 40 sessions of one-on-one work, depending on readiness.
v. Pricing

How pricing works

Each course is a three-session arc that does the complete work of a topic. Diagnosis, joint reading, then a piece of written or oral output. The per-session rate drops as the student commits to more courses. Cumulative work in the same hands compounds. After 20 to 40 sessions of one-on-one work, students unlock the seminar tier. Half the per-session rate, taken as a yearly practice. The seminar requires students who can hold their own in collective inquiry. The earlier sessions build that capacity.

Single sessions
$300
3 sessions · $100 each
The minimum to begin.
Five sessions
$575
$510
$102 each · 11% off
Extend without committing to a full bundle.
Seven-class bundle
$805
$630
$90 each · 22% off
The standard term-long bundle.
森林・私塾
The Forest year
$25,000
$14,400
144 contact hours · one-on-one
A full year inside the practice. Three sessions a week across all five subjects. A written account of the student's development at year-end, suitable for sharing with universities.
私塾・講座
The Seminar year
$12,500
$7,200
144 contact hours · small seminar
For students who have completed at least 20 sessions of one-on-one work. Same yearly practice in seminar format with a small group of prepared peers. Half the per-session rate of the Forest year, because seminar work makes one teaching session reach several students at once.
Unlocks at 20-40 one-on-one sessions
Cancellation and rescheduling

Cancel or reschedule for free up to twenty-four hours before a session. Inside twenty-four hours, the session is non-refundable, because the teacher has reserved that hour and cannot rebook it.

Once per term, every family gets a single late cancellation, no questions asked. If a teacher cancels for any reason, the session is refunded in full.

vi. Teachers

Who teaches

Saul Nassau teaches all five subjects directly. The other Leizu teachers form a small network, each one trained in the seminar method by Saul before joining. Each teacher takes a small number of students per term so the practice stays the practice.

Every Leizu teacher does their own ongoing study. They read widely. They write seriously. The seminar method requires that the teacher has done the work and continues doing it. A teacher who has stopped reading cannot host an inquiry.

vii. Common questions

FAQ 問

How do sessions work?
Each session runs around 90 minutes total. The structure is a focused 40 to 45 minute working period, a 5 to 10 minute break, then a second 40 to 45 minute working period. The break is structural rather than incidental. Sustained attention to a difficult text needs the consolidation interval to do what it does. We meet over Google Meet, generated automatically when you book. You will get the link by email with a calendar invite. No software to install. Click the link at session time.
In-person or only online?
Default is online. In-person is possible in the Toronto area for ongoing students; we work it out after the first few online sessions if it makes sense for both of us.
What ages do you teach?
Mostly high-school and university level. Some adults working on their own projects. The youngest student I've taught seriously was 12; the oldest is in their seventies. The work changes shape, the standard doesn't.
Do parents book for their kids, or do students book themselves?
Either works. Parents typically book for younger students; older students book themselves and parents pay separately. Either way, payment goes through Stripe and confirmation lands in whichever email you use to book.
What if I'm not sure which course is right for me?
Book a Forest Year intake call. It's 20 minutes, free, and we figure out together what kind of work would actually serve you. No commitment to enrol after.
Can I switch courses partway through?
Yes. The curriculum is treated as one body of work. If we're three sessions into a Philosophy course and you'd rather pivot to History, we pivot. The hours you've paid for don't expire.
What if I have to miss a session?
Cancel or reschedule with at least 72 hours notice and there's no charge. Three free cancels per month before the 72-hour rule applies. Full details in the Policies section above.
What payment methods do you take?
All major credit and debit cards, via Stripe. Payment happens before the first session. Receipts arrive by email automatically.
Are these courses for credit?
Some are aligned with the Ontario OSSD curriculum and IB Diploma syllabi. The OSSD-aligned courses can support school-credit work but do not grant credits independently. Your school of record does that. The seminar-format and standalone courses are for the work itself, not credit.
Do you teach ESL students?
Yes. ESL is one of the registers I work in. Teaching is in English at all levels, including IELTS and TOEFL prep. Communication with families is also available in Mandarin, French, or Farsi if needed.
What's the difference between one-on-one and seminar courses?
One-on-one is just you and me, fully customized. Seminar is a small group of students working through the same material together. Seminar courses require some foundation of one-on-one work first. Typically 20 to 40 sessions, depending on readiness. This is not a gate. It is because seminar work goes better when participants share a baseline.
What if I can't afford the tuition?
Apply to Leizu Scholars. The program waives tuition for students who can't currently afford it. Same standard, same work, no payment until you're financially able later in life.
How do I reach you with a question?
Text or call 942-380-4606, or email through the form below. Texting is the fastest path.
viii. Need-based

Leizu Scholars 嫘祖學者

Tuition is a real obstacle for some students. Leizu Scholars is a need-based program that waives tuition for students who can't currently afford it. The standard is the same. The work is the same. The only thing that's different is that you don't pay now.

If you'd like to apply, write a letter explaining your situation and what you want to study. If you'd like to support a scholar, the Mulberry Fund accepts donations directly.

Apply for a scholar seat → Support the Mulberry Fund →
ix. Contact

Contact

Courses are bookable directly through the catalog above. Email or text for any questions before or after booking. The free class via The Agora is available below for anyone who wants to see how the work happens before committing.

FREE · OPEN SEMINAR · NO REGISTRATION
Free class
Leizu does not offer paid trial classes. The Agora is the open seminar. Free. Open to all. The place to see how the work happens. Read the text. Show up.

One thing before booking

Read the policies once. They cover cancellations, no-shows, payment, and how the seminar tier unlocks. Tick the box below to confirm you have read them, then I will open your email client with the booking request pre-filled.
